column4: Structure / Shape
column6: Entry Trigger
column10: Trader Notes / Edge
column11: Example / Visual Description
column12: Volume Confirmation
column13: Session VP Confirmation

column4: HH-H-L-LL swing; price retests QML immediately after break
column6: Short at QML on first retest candle
column7: QML = Last Higher-Low before the LL
column10: Fast, aggressive retest; high-probability reversal if QML holds
column11: EURUSD 15m: Price breaks prior LL, immediately retests last HL (QML) with bearish pin bar. Short taken; 40-pip drop into demand.
column12: Volume spike + long upper wick rejection at QML confirms institutional supply. Decreasing volume on follow-through strengthens reversal. VP: Rejection at HVN or above POC inside VA = strong supply.
column13: Session VP: London or NY session POC rejection at QML = highest-conviction reversal. Avoid if inside Asian SVP Value Area.

column4: Same QM structure; retest occurs much later after LL
column6: Short at QML on delayed retest
column10: Slower formation; still valid; watch for IQM scenario
column11: GBPJPY 1H: Price drifts sideways 4 hrs then retests QML with doji rejection. 80-pip move lower.
column12: Moderate volume on retest (no spike) + lower volume on drift confirms weak buying. Avoid if volume expands on retest. VP: Retest at LVN outside VA = high-probability reversal.
column13: Session VP: Retest aligns with previous London SVP VAL or NY open LVN = accelerated move expected.

column4: Bearish/bullish candle cluster at QML (shadow pattern)
column6: Short on shadow candle confirmation at QML
column10: Shadow = institutional rejection candle; confirms supply
column11: AUDUSD 5m: Bearish cluster with long upper wicks at QML. Short on close; rapid reversal.
column12: Multiple rejection wicks with elevated volume = institutional selling cluster. VP: Shadow cluster at HVN or POC = strong institutional supply confirmation.
column13: Session VP: London open DM Shadow at London SVP POC = ultra-high probability. Avoid shadows forming inside Asian SVP VA mid-session.

column4: QM forms mid-trend; acts as continuation not reversal
column6: Enter at QML in direction of prevailing trend
column10: Distinguish from reversal QM by context — continuation QMs form inside trends
column11: NASDAQ 5m uptrend: Small QM forms inside trend; QML holds as support. Long in trend direction.
column12: Volume should contract at QML retest then re-expand on continuation. VP: QML aligns with VAL or POC inside up-trending structure = trend continuation confirmed.
column13: Session VP: Continuation QM at NY SVP VAL (bull) or VAH (bear) = strongest trend continuation filter; momentum favors trend.

column4: Price sweeps small QML then returns to main QML for entry
column6: Enter at Main QML after small QML is taken
column10: Two-tier QM; small QML is a fakeout inducing premature longs
column11: USDJPY 15m: Small QML swept (traps longs), returns to main QML for clean rejection.
column12: Volume spike on small QML sweep = stop hunt confirmed; volume decreases on return to main QML = absorption. VP: Main QML at HVN = distribution zone; small QML sweep = retail trap.
column13: Session VP: Small QML sweep during Asian session + main QML rejection at London SVP POC = textbook institutional re-entry.

column4: QML tested but not respected; price pushes through, sets trap
column6: Wait for fakeout above IQML then reverse
column10: IQM signals failed supply; fakeout entry traps shorts
column11: EURGBP 1H: QML broken aggressively (fakeout), price reverses sharply from demand. Short after sweep.
column12: Volume expansion through IQML (fakeout) then immediate volume collapse = institutional trap confirmed. VP: IQML break through LVN then reversal from HVN above = failed auction.
column13: Session VP: Fakeout through IQML occurs in low-volume Asian session; reversal at London open SVP POC = highest-conviction IQM entry.

column4: Double resistance test; price fakes above R-R zone then reverses
column6: Short after second failed breakout above R-R
column10: R-R = double supply test; 2R/2S is exhaustion signal
column11: SPX 5m: Price breaks R1 then R2 (stop hunt), both fail with long upper wicks. Short on second rejection.
column12: Volume on second R test must be lower than first — divergence = exhaustion. VP: Both R tests at or above HVN = strong institutional supply ceiling.
column13: Session VP: Second resistance test at NY SVP VAH = distribution confirmed. Avoid if second test occurs outside session VA during low-volume periods.

column4: Two V-shaped reversals at same support; forms double bottom variant
column6: Long after second V-bottom holds support
column10: Two sequential selling climaxes; second is weaker — institutional absorption
column11: GBPUSD 30m: Two sharp V-bottoms at same demand zone; second has lower volume. Long taken; strong rally.
column12: Second V-bottom must show lower volume than first = absorption confirmed; volume expansion on reversal candle = institutional buy. VP: V-bottoms at HVN or POC = demand floor validated.
column13: Session VP: V Twin forming at London SVP VAL or NY SVP VAL = session demand floor; high-probability long setup during active session hours.

column4: Consolidation channel after impulse; price breaks supply/demand at flag limit
column6: Enter on break of Flag Limit (supply or demand)
column10: Flag B is a single flag; R1/R2 = internal flag structure levels
column11: EURUSD 5m uptrend: Impulse up, tight flag consolidation; breaks flag limit with engulfing candle.
column12: Volume contraction inside flag + expansion on flag limit breakout = classic institutional accumulation. VP: Breakout above flag through LVN = fast momentum move; stall at HVN = partial target.
column13: Session VP: Flag B breakout through NY SVP VAH (bull) = momentum continuation into NY session. London SVP flag breakout = strongest forex continuation signal.

column4: Two-flag sequence; Flag A sets direction, Flag B continues
column6: Enter at Flag B Limit in direction of Flag A break
column7: Flag A & B limits; S-S-S demand zone
column10: Combined flags amplify momentum; high-conviction continuation setup
column11: NASDAQ 15m: Flag A breaks up, followed immediately by Flag B; clean break of Flag B limit. Strong long.
column12: Volume: contraction in both flags + strongest expansion on Flag B breakout = institutional load confirmed. VP: Flag B breaks through LVN = air pocket for fast measured move.
column13: Session VP: Flag A+B sequence straddling London/NY session open = cross-session momentum; strongest when Flag B breaks NY SVP VAH.

column4: Horizontal line connecting equal highs or lows (2-3-3 count)
column6: Trade at Ruler level on retest
column10: Ruler = institutional order line; 2-3-3 count confirms significance
column11: USDJPY 1H: Three equal highs form horizontal ruler line; price retests and rejects. Short at ruler.
column12: Each touch of ruler with diminishing volume = institutional absorption. VP: Ruler aligns with HVN = confirmed institutional order cluster; 3+ touches at HVN = do not fade.
column13: Session VP: Ruler level aligning with London SVP POC or NY SVP VAH/VAL = ultra-precise institutional level; session-specific POC at ruler = highest conviction.

column4: Price tests the same S/R level multiple times (stop hunt sequence)
column6: Enter after stop hunt candle / engulf at MPL
column10: Each touch = liquidity grab; engulf confirms reversal from MPL
column11: GBPJPY 15m: Same support touched 4 times with stop hunts; final engulfing candle reverses. Long entered.
column12: Diminishing volume on successive MPL tests = absorption in progress; volume surge on final engulf = reversal confirmed. VP: MPL at HVN = defended by institutions; LVN break above/below = fast exit.
column13: Session VP: Final MPL stop hunt occurring at London SVP VAL or NY SVP VAL = session low defended; reversal into session VA = high-probability long.

column4: Price breaks R1 then R2, both fail; reversal into S zone
column6: Short after R2 fakeout closes back inside range
column10: Classic stop-hunt above double resistance; default fakeout pattern
column11: SPX 5m: Breaks R1 then R2 (liquidity sweep), immediately closes back inside. Clean drop.
column12: Volume expansion on R1/R2 break = stop hunt fuel; volume collapse on close back inside = fakeout confirmed. VP: R2 break into LVN above then retreat = no buyers above HVN.
column13: Session VP: Fakeout above NY SVP VAH (indices) = distribution; short on close back below VAH. London SVP VAH fakeout = primary forex fakeout signal.

column4: Support flips to resistance after break; price retests from below
column6: Short at prior S now acting as R after SR Flip
column10: Support-turned-resistance entry; confirmation is rejection candle at flip
column11: EURUSD 30m: Prior support broken, retested from below as resistance with pin bar. Short entered.
column12: Volume expansion on support break + contraction on retest = SR flip confirmed; rejection candle with elevated volume at flip = institutional supply. VP: SR Flip at POC = former fair value now resistance.
column13: Session VP: SR Flip aligns with London SVP POC (new resistance) = session-specific supply confirmed; NY SVP SR Flip = strong intraday signal for indices.

column4: QM head at same level as R1/R2 or S1/S2 — creates diamond pattern
column6: Trade at diamond apex (QM head = prior S/R)
column7: QM head level = R1/R2 or S1/S2
column10: Diamond = dual confluence; QM + SR alignment is strong institutional signal
column11: GBPUSD 15m: QM head exactly aligns with R2 forming perfect diamond. Reversal short at apex.
column12: Volume divergence between QM head and R1/R2 = distribution. VP: Diamond apex at HVN = institutional ceiling; aligned supply at same level = non-negotiable resistance.
column13: Session VP: Diamond apex coinciding with NY SVP HVN or London SVP POC = strongest fakeout confirmation; dual confluence of pattern + session VP = institutional trap.

column4: Diamond variant using Support-Becomes-Resistance (SBR) flip
column6: Short at SBR after diamond forms
column10: SBR version confirms failed breakout + QM structure convergence
column11: AUDUSD 1H: Diamond forms at SBR flip level. Short taken; strong bearish follow-through.
column12: Volume contraction on SBR retest + rejection = supply confirmed at former support. VP: SBR at POC/HVN = former fair value becomes resistance; strongest when POC flips with price.
column13: Session VP: SBR at London SVP POC (forex) or NY SVP VAH (indices) = session-specific resistance validated; avoid trading SBR inside Asian SVP VA.

column4: Two sequential supply tests; price forms double top at supply
column6: Short on second supply test / rejection
column10: Second test of supply with equal or weaker momentum = distribution signal
column11: SPX 30m: Two equal highs at supply zone; second high shows weaker momentum. Short entered.
column12: Volume on second supply test must be lower than first = distribution confirmed; avoid if second test has higher volume. VP: Both tops at HVN = institutional ceiling clearly defended.
column13: Session VP: Second supply test at NY SVP VAH (indices) = double distribution confirmed; London SVP VAH second test = primary forex double-top signal.

column4: Three progressive drives in same direction; third = exhaustion
column6: Enter counter-trend after 3rd drive at S/D zone
column10: 3 Drives = Elliott impulse variant; 3rd drive often forms at key supply or demand
column11: EURUSD 1H downtrend: Three progressive lower drives into demand; third exhausts. Bullish reversal long.
column12: Each successive drive with lower volume = exhaustion in progress; volume surge on 3rd drive reversal candle = institutional absorption. VP: 3rd drive terminating at HVN = strong demand floor.
column13: Session VP: 3rd drive completing at London SVP VAL (forex) or NY SVP VAL (futures) = session demand floor; highest-probability reversal when 3rd drive exhausts inside session hours.

column4: Price coils in tightening range above demand or below supply
column6: Enter on compression break; confirm with engulf
column10: Compression = spring-loading; break is often explosive; wait for clear candle
column11: GBPUSD 5m: Price coils tightly above demand; breaks upper limit with strong engulfing candle. Long taken.
column12: Declining volume inside compression = coiling confirmed; volume explosion on breakout candle = spring released. VP: Breakout through LVN = fast momentum; stall at HVN = partial profit.
column13: Session VP: Compression breaking through NY SVP LVN at open = explosive session momentum. London open compression break = strongest forex compression signal.

column4: Compression with embedded liquidity grab (LQ) before break
column6: Enter after liquidity sweep below compression (bull) or above (bear)
column10: LQ = stop-hunt within compression; entry after sweep + engulf is highest-prob
column11: NASDAQ 15m: Tight compression with quick LQ sweep below, then explosive upside break. Long entered.
column12: Volume spike on LQ sweep (stop hunt) + immediate contraction + expansion on breakout = triple volume confirmation. VP: LQ sweep below VAL inside compression then break above POC = strongest CPLQ signal.
column13: Session VP: LQ sweep occurring in final Asian session minutes + London open breakout through London SVP LVN = textbook cross-session CPLQ; highest-probability entry of all compression variants.

column4: Extended sideways chop at S/D zone; accumulation/distribution
column6: Enter on break from Can-Can zone
column10: Can-Can = institutional accumulation; narrow range = coiled spring before impulse
column11: USDJPY 30m: 2-hour sideways chop at demand zone (Can-Can); clean upside break. Long taken.
column12: Consistently low volume inside Can-Can = accumulation; volume explosion on zone break = institutional participation. VP: Can-Can zone aligning with VA = institutional fair value accumulation zone.
column13: Session VP: Can-Can zone spanning multiple sessions; break during London/NY open = session-fueled momentum. Asian SVP Can-Can break often signals London continuation.

column4: Can-Can zone with fakeout above (Fake Supply) or below (Fake Demand)
column6: Enter after fakeout reversal from Fake Supply/Demand
column7: LQ zone; Fake Supply / Fake Demand
column10: Fakeout within Can-Can confirms stop hunt; strongest variant of the pattern
column11: GBPJPY 15m: Can-Can zone with fakeout below (liquidity grab); immediate reversal up. Strong long.
column12: Volume spike on fakeout wick + collapse on return into zone = stop hunt confirmed. VP: Fakeout into LVN outside VA then reversal back into VA = failed auction = strongest Can-Can variant.
column13: Session VP: Can-Can fakeout occurring at London open into London SVP LVN, then reversal into London SVP VA = session-specific failed auction; ultra-high probability entry.

column4: Left shoulder-head-right shoulder; neckline
column6: Short on neckline break or retest
column10: Popular swing trader reversal; confluence with supply/QM
column11: SPX Daily: Classic H&S at major supply; neckline break with volume. Short taken; measured move hits target.
column12: Volume on head must be higher than left shoulder; right shoulder lower volume = distribution. Neckline break with volume expansion = confirmed. VP: Head at HVN; neckline break through LVN = fast measured move.
column13: Session VP: H&S completing during NY session with right shoulder at NY SVP VAH = session distribution; neckline break through NY SVP LVN = accelerated downside.

column4: Inverted shoulders-head; neckline
column6: Long on neckline break or retest
column10: Bullish reversal at demand zones
column11: EURUSD Weekly: Inverse H&S at macro demand; neckline break. Long entered; strong rally.
column12: Volume on head must exceed left shoulder; right shoulder with lower volume = accumulation. Neckline break with expansion = confirmed. VP: Inverted head at HVN (demand); neckline break through LVN = momentum.
column13: Session VP: Inverse H&S completing at London SVP VAL (forex) = session demand floor reversal; neckline break during NY open through NY SVP LVN = cross-session momentum.

column4: Rounded cup + handle consolidation
column6: Long on handle breakout above resistance
column10: Swing trader accumulation continuation pattern (O'Neil/Minervini)
column11: AAPL Daily: Rounded cup, tight handle; breakout above resistance. Long taken.
column12: Volume declines through cup and handle; explosive volume on handle breakout = accumulation complete. VP: Handle base at POC or VAL; breakout through VAH into LVN = fast continuation.
column13: Session VP: Cup & Handle on daily; handle breakout coinciding with NY open SVP VAH break = strongest swing continuation signal; institutional participation confirmed by NY session volume.

column4: Upward converging trendlines
column6: Short on break below lower trendline
column10: Exhaustion in uptrend; day/swing favorite
column11: TSLA 1H: Rising wedge in uptrend; breaks lower trendline with momentum. Short entered.
column12: Volume should decrease as wedge narrows = exhaustion. Volume expansion on lower trendline break = reversal confirmed. VP: Wedge high at HVN/POC; break below through LVN = fast drop.
column13: Session VP: Rising wedge peak at NY SVP VAH (indices) = session exhaustion; lower trendline break during NY session = intraday reversal with momentum. London SVP wedge peak = forex signal.

column4: Downward converging trendlines
column6: Long on break above upper trendline
column10: Bullish reversal in downtrend
column11: BTC 4H: Falling wedge in downtrend; breaks upper trendline. Long taken; sharp reversal.
column12: Volume diminishes as wedge narrows = exhaustion. Volume expansion on upper trendline break = reversal confirmed. VP: Wedge low at HVN (demand); break above through LVN = spring release.
column13: Session VP: Falling wedge completing at London SVP VAL (forex) = session demand floor; upper trendline break during London/NY overlap = strongest falling wedge signal.

column4: Long wick rejection at level
column6: Enter opposite wick on confirmation
column10: Institutional rejection; aligns with DM Shadow; day-trader favorite
column11: EURUSD 5m: Long upper wick pin bar at resistance. Short on close; immediate reversal.
column12: Pin bar with elevated volume + long wick = institutional rejection candle. VP: Pin bar forming at HVN or POC = strong supply/demand confirmed; wick piercing LVN then closing back = failed breakout.
column13: Session VP: Pin bar at London SVP POC (forex) or NY SVP POC (indices) = session-specific rejection; highest conviction when pin wick pierces session HVN then closes back inside VA.

column4: Small bearish + larger bullish engulf
column6: Long above engulfing high
column10: Strong reversal candle at support for day traders
column11: GBPUSD 15m: Small red candle followed by large green engulfing at demand. Long entered.
column12: Bullish engulf with volume > prior bearish candle = institutional buy confirmed. VP: Engulfing at HVN or POC = demand floor institutional confirmation.
column13: Session VP: Bullish engulfing at NY SVP VAL (indices/futures) = NY session demand defended; London SVP VAL engulfing = forex session low confirmed; strong continuation expected.

column4: Last bearish candle before strong bullish move
column6: Long on retest of OB
column10: Institutional demand; level-to-level entry; core SMC tool
column11: NAS100 5m: Last bearish candle before strong impulse up; price retests OB. Long taken.
column12: OB forms with high volume bearish candle then explosive bullish move = institutional accumulation candle. Retest with lower volume = demand holds. VP: OB aligns with HVN or VAL = demand zone validated.
column13: Session VP: OB at NY SVP VAL (futures/indices) = NY session order block demand; retest during NY open = highest-probability OB entry. London SVP VAL OB = primary forex OB signal.

column4: 3-candle price imbalance/gap
column6: Trade in bias direction or fill
column10: Imbalance fill or momentum tool for structured traders
column11: EURJPY 15m: Clear 3-candle FVG formed; price fills gap then continues in trend. Momentum trade taken.
column12: FVG forms on high-volume impulse candle = institutional imbalance. Fill with lower volume = unmitigated. VP: FVG coincides with LVN = price fills rapidly; FVG at HVN = partial fill expected.
column13: Session VP: FVG formed during London open impulse = London SVP imbalance; fill during NY session. NY session FVG = highest-volume imbalance; fills before end of NY session 80%+ of the time.

column4: Break prior swing high/low in trend dir.
column6: Trend direction entry on pullback
column10: Confirms trend; core structured analysis
column11: SPX 30m: Price breaks previous swing high with strong candle. Trend continuation long on pullback.
column12: BOS candle must show volume expansion = institutional participation in trend. Pullback to broken swing on low volume = continuation setup. VP: BOS breaks through LVN = momentum; stalls at HVN = pullback entry.
column13: Session VP: BOS occurring at NY open through NY SVP VAH (bull) = session trend confirmation; London BOS through London SVP VAH = forex trend day signal; highest conviction in active session.

column4: Break opposite swing (reversal signal)
column6: Counter-trend after confirmation
column10: Structure shift signal for bias change
column11: GBPJPY 1H: Price breaks opposite swing low (CHOCH); bias flips bearish. Short entered.
column12: CHOCH break on high volume = institutional participation in reversal. Follow-through with continued volume expansion = trend change confirmed. VP: CHOCH break through LVN = accelerated reversal; stalls at HVN = continuation needed.
column13: Session VP: CHOCH during London open through London SVP opposite VA side = session bias flip; NY open CHOCH through NY SVP opposite boundary = highest-conviction reversal for indices/futures.

column4: Sweep equal H/L or stops then reverse
column6: Enter reversal after sweep + candle
column10: Stop hunt fuel; day & institutional
column11: AUDUSD 5m: Quick sweep of equal lows (stops), then strong reversal candle. Long entered.
column12: Volume spike on sweep wick = stop hunt confirmed; immediate volume collapse + reversal candle = institutional absorption. VP: Sweep into LVN below VA then reversal = failed auction = strongest liquidity sweep signal.
column13: Session VP: Liquidity sweep at London open below Asian SVP VAL = cross-session stop hunt; reversal into London SVP VA = London session long bias confirmed. NY sweep of London SVP VAL = NY open reversal.

column4: Impulse A-B, consolidation, equal C-D
column6: Breakout of consolidation in impulse dir.
column10: Level-to-level target; structured projection; day/swing staple
column11: USDJPY 15m: Strong impulse leg, consolidation, then equal measured move leg completes. Target hit precisely.
column12: Volume expansion on first impulse + contraction in consolidation + re-expansion on measured leg = projection confirmed. VP: Breakout above VAH or through HVN confirms measured target validity.
column13: Session VP: Breakout through NY SVP VAH or London SVP HVN = strongest measured-move confirmation; target often reaches opposite session VA boundary.
